Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The building crusaders come to the aid of 12-year-old Matthew from Telford, Shropshire, who has been stuck in hospital for three years after suffering cancer and a stroke. The youngsters head teacher and school friends have been fundraising to get him home, so now, Nick Knowles and the team - with the help of hundreds of volunteers - set out to make Matthews home suitable for his complex medical needs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: As part of the 2013 appeal, Nick Knowles and the team head to Peterborough to help Children in Need-funded organisation Little Miracles, which supports youngsters with disabilities and life-limiting illnesses and their families. However, whereas Mark, Chris, Julian and Billy normally renovate an existing property, for the first time they are knocking down a building and reconstructing it from the ground up - making it worth almost £1million. With just nine days to complete the job, theyll need help - and lots of it. Luckily, tradespeople turn out in force - from both the local area and across the country - giving their time for free

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In the summer of 2015, West Sussex wife and mother-of-four Amanda fell off her bike while training for an endurance contest, breaking 11 bones, puncturing a lung, and snapping her collarbone and back. Paralysed, she spent the next six months at Stoke Mandevilles spinal unit, while her builder husband Vic looked after the children - but determined to compete again, she returned home in a wheelchair. However, the family home is no longer suitable for Amanda - so thats where Nick Knowles and the rest of the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a jackpot

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Great Dane is rushed to Fitzpatrick Referrals after losing the use of its back legs due to a slipped disc, with complications leaving the dog fighting for its life. A woman and her two children bring their pet spaniel to the practice after it is hit by a car, and a 10-year-old collie with hip dysplasia visits the surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick tends to a Newfoundland called Billy, who clocks up thousands of miles travelling back and forth from his home in Aberdeen for treatment on its legs. He also attempts to help Barney, a Yorkshire terrier that has a deformity in its neck that is compressing its spinal cord. For owners Linda and Barry, Noel is their last chance, but the operation involves high-risk surgery with no guarantee of success

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The building crusaders come to the aid of 12-year-old Matthew from Telford, Shropshire, who has been stuck in hospital for three years after suffering cancer and a stroke. The youngsters head teacher and school friends have been fundraising to get him home, so now, Nick Knowles and the team - with the help of hundreds of volunteers - set out to make Matthews home suitable for his complex medical needs

With just nine days to complete the job, theyll need help - and lots of it. Luckily, tradespeople turn out in force - from both the local area and across the country - giving their time for free

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In this 100th episode, the crews go cross country after a teenagers bike crash, attend a man whos had a nasty fall and help a woman with end-stage cancer

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Paramedic Kim climbs into a crushed car balanced over a fast-flowing river, while services across the Pennines are paralysed when a man plunges from a bridge onto a busy rail line

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: More comedy sketches, including Nan visiting a pound shop, and Lauren discovering she has not been invited to a party

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Staceys brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best mans speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.sub-title: The Nine Lives of Teddy Hart
programme.desc: Teddy meets aspiring wrestler Samantha Fiddler and takes her to Florida to train, but when he returns to Canada, Samanthas family reports her missing

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off about the police sting by his stepmother. Stuart voices his concern that his colleague is letting personal involvement with the family cloud his judgement - an opinion borne out when Max confronts the armed youngster without telling anybody of his plans. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max, Grace and Jacob arrest a drug dealer as he makes a deal but his runner escapes, his face concealed by a hoodie. The investigation takes a serious turn when the youngster crashes the stolen car he is driving and runs off, leaving a passenger fighting for her life. The unidentified fugitive is eventually traced to a bail hostel, where three possible suspects are taken in for questioning, but a false confession makes the detectives job doubly difficult

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The resident magician of a social club turns to crime to fund a gambling habit and falls prey to a blackmailer threatening to harm his girlfriend. Neil and Stevie persuade the hapless entertainer to take part in a sting operation at the club but the suspect under surveillance becomes involved in a brawl and the money vanishes into thin air

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Beth shocks Tony by revealing she has applied for a transfer, but before they can discuss it further they spot a woman preparing to jump to her death from a bridge over the River Thames. Nate eventually rescues her from the water, only to find she is carrying a bag of heroin. Investigations into the source of the drugs focus on the boxing club where she works

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Beth prepares for her last day at Sun Hill, her colleagues urge her to show her feelings for Nate at her leaving party. She is pleased to find herself paired up with him on a call about a domestic disturbance in which a woman is found unconscious at the bottom of the stairs. The officers are surprised to discover their prime suspect is the boxing club owner they interviewed the previous day about drug dealing on his premises

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A lorry driver left for dead by masked raiders who hijacked his vehicle in an isolated lay-by admits to smuggling a woman and her child into the country to help them escape a violent marriage. The search is on to locate the stolen vehicle but when it is finally traced and thoroughly searched, Mel suspects her colleague Arun has tipped off the fugitives, abusing his position as a police officer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Leon and Callum investigate the return of stolen goods to the house from which they were taken - apparently by a remorseful burglar. They discover their suspect is covering for his drug addict sister, who cannot escape the clutches of her violent dealer, but in the ensuing sting operation £300 goes missing and suspicion falls on Leon himself

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground

programme.desc: A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping

programme.desc: Join ambulances, helicopters and boats across New Zealand, transporting critical patients to safety. Paramedics find a motocross rider near death in a remote forest

programme.desc: A man is at risk of losing his leg after a horrific accident with a boat propeller, while another needs to be airlifted to hospital after an accident involving a tractor

programme.desc: A man is at risk of losing his leg after a horrific accident with a boat propeller, while another needs to be airlifted to hospital after an accident involving a tractor

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a momentous trip to the church for the reading of the banns, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding. Elsewhere, the early return of Staceys brother Jason horrifies Uncle Bryn. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Staceys brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best mans speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: More comedy sketches, including Nan visiting a pound shop, and Lauren discovering she has not been invited to a party

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.desc: With Samanthas family desperate for news, Teddy answers Fredericks questions. As new information comes to light, one of Teddys ex-students speaks up

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien come across a water trough and a cartwheel as they continue their mission to turn unwanted items into hard cash

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy explores how American history has been mythologised and manipulated by generations of politicians, writers and protesters. She begins by examining the American Revolution - a David-and-Goliath battle of men with high ideals taking on the might of the British Empire. But how much of Americas founding story is based on fact?

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos railway journey through the Midlands and West Country takes him to Coventry, where he recalls the destruction by the German Luftwaffe of the citys gothic cathedral in November 1940. He visits the factory of the London Electric Vehicle Company, manufacturers of the iconic London taxi, and then heads south to Royal Leamington Spa and the national centre for the Guide Dogs for the Blind

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Warwickshire, where he gets a glimpse of the work being done for the new HS2 railway infrastructure. In Stratford-upon-Avon he learns about the founding of the Royal Shakespeare Company, before a visit to Birmingham, where he recalls the redevelopment of the city centre during the 1960s and the arrival of black and Asian communities

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo stops in Wolverhampton, where he finds out about the impact of Enoch Powells 1968 speech. He also visits Kingsford Country Park on the outskirts of Kidderminster, investigates pigeon-racing in Worcester, and eventually looks into Cheltenhams jazz festival

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Tewkesbury in Gloucestershire, Michael Portillo heads to the castle of Eastnor at the foot of the Malvern Hills. He also visits Coleford in the Forest of Dean, Slimbridge in Gloucestershire, and Bristol Temple Meads. From there he heads to Filton to investigate the centre of Britains post-war aviation industry

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks grandchildren, Jack and Amy, head to the North East of England to pick up some charming old bikes. Derek helps a collector downsize and discovers a rare Chrysler Alpine

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommels Afrika Korps and Montgomerys Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A look at Wernher von Braun, the gifted young scientist whose ambition led him to abandon any sense of morality in pursuit of his dream of space flight

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Sefton Park in Liverpool, where treasures include a medal given to one of the first men to land on D-Day, a dress by Swinging Sixties fashion designer Ossie Clark, and one of the first vials of penicillin. Fiona also meets the son of a Chinese immigrant and ship worker to discuss the history of the Chinese community in Liverpool

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce visits one of the grandest houses in Wales, Powis Castle. Treasures include memorabilia from the 1966 World Cup, a statue of a lifeboatman and a medal awarded for the rescue of a man who fell into a crevasse. Runjeet Singh, a specialist in Asian arms and militaria, tells Fiona about Tipu Sultan, the 18th-century Indian ruler known as the Tiger of Mysore, and how objects from his treasury ended up in Powis Castle after they were looted by British troops

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team explore whether one wheel fewer means cheaper bills as they take on a three-wheeled wonder, the Reliant Robin

programme.desc: The team dive into the wartime history books and uncover a Harley Davidson 42WLA

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommels Afrika Korps and Montgomerys Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A look at Wernher von Braun, the gifted young scientist whose ambition led him to abandon any sense of morality in pursuit of his dream of space flight

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Sefton Park in Liverpool, where treasures include a medal given to one of the first men to land on D-Day, a dress by Swinging Sixties fashion designer Ossie Clark, and one of the first vials of penicillin. Fiona also meets the son of a Chinese immigrant and ship worker to discuss the history of the Chinese community in Liverpool

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer embarks on a journey across the country, meeting a diverse array of people and encountering exotic wildlife. He begins in Northern Territory, where he discovers how the camel is threatening the fragile desert ecosystem, then heads through wine country to the South Australian city of Port Lincoln to dive with critically endangered southern blue fin tuna. In Western Australia, he joins a 21st-century gold rush and ends his first leg in state capital Perth, where he meets a former binman from Hull who now has a house in the sun, a private pool and his own boat

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher helps cellmate Godber make it through the night, calling on his unpredictable and limitless imagination to while away the hours. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates face a day of hard labour digging drains on a lonely stretch of moorland. However, Fletcher soon finds a way of turning the situation to his advantage. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch desperately wants to get out of the tedious task of making fishing nets, and hatches a crafty scheme to get himself assigned to the library. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team explore whether one wheel fewer means cheaper bills as they take on a three-wheeled wonder, the Reliant Robin

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien discover items including baby scales, an oil can and a glass cabinet that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley debunks the myths behind the American Civil War, revealing how its history has been retold over the years to promote freedom and equality but also hatred and division. She reveals how Abraham Lincolns personal views, and the behaviour of his troops towards African Americans, were not as noble as they appeared. She also learns how history was rewritten in a bid to downplay the evils of slavery, and how a 1905 blockbuster film about the Civil War relaunched the Ku Klux Klan with terrifying results

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Scottish railway journey reaches Aberdeen. From the granite city, Michael heads out to Forvie National Nature Reserve, which is 1,000 hectares of protected coastal habitat dominated by grassy dunes

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the Culloden Viaduct aboard the Caledonian Sleeper to arrive bright and early in Inverness, from where he heads north on a magnificent scenic adventure to the Orkney archipelago. On boggy moorland overlooking the Moray Firth near Inverness, Michael explores the battlefield at Culloden, where Bonnie Prince Charlies Jacobite army fought British Government troops in 1746

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by train north from Inverness to the Orkney Islands. Beginning on the Moray Firth, Michael admires the Cullen Viaduct then enjoys a bowl of Cullen skink at a family-run fishmonger of 150 years standing. In the factory smokery, he learns from the owner how the famous soup acquires its distinctive taste

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Scottish coastal railway journey reaches Invergordon on the Cromarty Firth, the third great inlet on the northeast coast of the Highlands. He arrives on Britains northernmost railway, the Far North Line, and heads straight onto the water to explore a former oil drilling rig now moored in the firth

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis clears a barn full of old treasures. His buys include a weathervane and the wooden sides of a horse-drawn cart, which undergo an amazing reinvention

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary goes hell-for-leather organising an antiques fair. He buys some military tripods and a vintage truck grille and repurposes them as stunning pieces of furniture

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Client Mark has always dreamed of owning a chrome-bumpered MGB convertible, but fixers Sheldon Nichols and Peter Harriott must find clever ways to finance that dream

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Driving instructor Brian loved his Fiat 500 until it rusted away - now Sheldon and Peter must come up with a dream car for no more than £10,000 to make any money

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch desperately wants to get out of the tedious task of making fishing nets, and hatches a crafty scheme to get himself assigned to the library. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming explores the Victorian vWest Yorkshire village of Saltaire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. He also moors up in Leeds and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom finishes the summer with new refurbished windows before he and Sam head to St Agnes to buy Cornish tin to cast a workshop sign

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: In the third part of his journey, Simon travels through the Andes mountains from Peru to Bolivia, beginning at the world-famous ruins of Machu Pichu. He then visits a remote valley where the mostly indigenous population farms coca leaves. the primary ingredient of cocaine. Heading across the border into Bolivia, Simon discovers how the indigenous women known as cholas have battled for increasing influence and representation

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: On the fourth leg of his journey through the continent, Simon travels through three of the worlds most extreme environments as he takes in the salt flats of Bolivia, Brazils Pantanal wetland region and Paraguays Chaco Forest. In Bolivia, he meets a family making a living carving salt from the vast white expanse of the Uyuni flats, while in Brazil he has a close encounter with South Americas apex predator, the jaguar. In Paraguay, Simon visits a Mennonites school, where traditionally clothed children are drilled in Bible texts and there are no smartphones to be seen

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor operates one of the worlds biggest hydraulic cranes, gets behind the wheel of a tank, and discovers the story behind the invention of the hovercraft

programme.desc: Charlie picks up an iconic 60s motor - a Ford Cortina Deluxe - and a 1970s Morris van brings a taste of Carry On Camping to Pickering

programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls on the East Coast to help a collector who is saying goodbye to his prized Excelsior Consort and his handmade Yorkshire Coble boat

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he cant remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The train on which Marian is travelling crashes, trapping her in the wreckage. Although she cannot escape, shes free to move around the carriage to help those injured, assisted by fellow passenger Dr Nick Burnett - who turns out to be the Royals new locum. Back at the hospital, a shortage of staff puts the casualty department under pressure when the crash victims begin to arrive. Damian OHare joins the cast, and Gerard Horan guest stars

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The boxer talks about his life inside and outside the ring. From growing up as a triplet to when he became the first Irishman to win an Olympic Gold medal in boxing in 1992

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as four new stars - reality TV star Ekin-Su Culculoglu, actor and comedian Hugh Dennis, author and interiors expert Kelly Hoppen, and former footballer Shaun Wright-Phillips - and their mentors, cook for their loved ones. Theyll have to produce delicious dishes with the added pressure of not only impressing the chefs, but their friends and family who will also be in the studio including Hughs friend David Baddiel

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2012

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the Derry legend, who discusses his colourful football career and the place GAA has in society

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian returns to Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London, meeting Spencer the stray beagle, who is consigned to quarantine with a contagious virus and petrified of humans. Paul also meets Lydia, the Hungarian Puli, a heavily dreadlocked rare pedigree, who is struggling in the hot summer heat, and Tintin the tearaway Yorkshire terrier who he takes for a trip to the hairdressers

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian meets three dogs in need of companionship. First up is excitable 10-month-old mongrel Blake, who loves playing with his toys - but when Paul suspects his eyesight is not as it should be and the vets confirm his fears, the presenter is given the challenge of finding the canine a suitable friend to act as his very own guide dog. Paul also meets Jacko the Jack Russell, who has been down in the dumps ever since arriving at Battersea, and 14-week-old Saluki Forest, who is in quarantine due to a stomach bug. Paul tries to come up with a way of keeping the puppy entertained - but with no physical contact allowed, that proves easier said than done

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons attempt to impress cheerleader Lily Amelia with their choice of outfit from Leeds city centre - which of them will be successful and be chosen for a prized date?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The five-time Paralympian talks about her life as an elite swimmer and her journey to gold in Rio 2016

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates the documentary series revealing the incredible ways in which all life is impacted throughout the four seasons

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Boxing promoter Kellie Maloney throws a dinner party for four strangers at her Essex home. Brian McFadden hosts the game show where diners try to guess the identity of a mystery host holding a dinner party in order to win a cash prize

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade investigates the mysterious deaths of fishermen in Alaska. A recent fatal incident sends him on a search for possible suspects - first through treacherous rapids, then down to the river-mouth where the icy waters are swarming with sharks. Yet its an unexpected beast, hauled up from the depths, that eventually emerges as his deadliest contender

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Wendy, Jack, Susheel and Neil pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: An argument threatens to ignite on a crew night out, and when the drinking continues back at the boat the drama spirals out of control

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The sports broadcaster talks to Tommy Martin about his childhood, his life as a footballer and his successful career as a journalist, radio host and TV pundit

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at the impact of summer in the northern hemisphere and winter in the south, revealing that wildlife faces feast, famine, fire and torrential rain in different regions around the world. On Marion Island life has frozen to a standstill, with king penguin chicks facing starvation as their parents left months ago, while in northern Australia its the dry season, and hooded parrots raise their chicks in termite mounds

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian and chat-show host presents a new selection of shaggy dog stories as he gets to know the canine residents of Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London. Paul meets a Dalmatian cross who has been abandoned - not long before giving birth. He also helps a hugely overweight shar-pei cross get back into shape and has his heartstrings pulled by a Yorkshire terrier which was found roaming Battersea Park

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian meets more of the canine residents of Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London, including a German shepherd puppy that was rejected by its mother at birth and a frisky Jack Russell that is having trouble using one of its back legs. Paul also comes to the aid of a shell-shocked old-timer that has been dumped outside the home and now needs help regaining a little confidence

programme.title: The Royal
programme.sub-title: All the Fun of the Fair
programme.desc: The residents are pleased when a fair comes to Elsinby, but Jeffrey and Samantha have their work cut out when the waltzer operator becomes trapped in the rides mechanism. Hes rushed to the hospital, where Mr Rose operates, and reveals his father has been suffering dizzy spells recently. A concerned Jeffrey heads back to the fairground, but hes too late to prevent disaster. Meanwhile, Ken hatches a dubious money-making scheme and Jill is forced to confront a patients wife. Paul Fox, Anna Madeley and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A group of drunken mods arrive at the hospital in a shocking state, and Ken Hopkirk soon makes the grim discovery that they have been drinking industrial alcohol supplied by their friend, theatre technician Alun Morris. Meanwhile, a fourth member of the boozing group is brought in and has his stomach pumped immediately - but it soon emerges that the illicit hooch has blinded him. Elsewhere, Mr Rose and his surgical team treat a widow with a gangrenous toe at her farmhouse, and student nurse Samantha Beaumont receives a telling-off for laying out operating instruments in an incorrect order. Andy Wear, Michael Starke and Robert Daws star, with a guest appearance by Thelma Barlow

programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: Paul OGradys Animal Orphans
programme.desc: The comedian begins another tour of Zambia, Namibia and South Africa helping to hand-rear orphaned animals and prepare them for release back into their natural habitats. Next, he teaches a young aardvark how to hunt for termites, before providing some much-needed company for a lion cub which was raised as a pet until she got too big to handle

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team.

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: An emergency call comes in to the stroke unit at Bronglais Hospital, Aberystwyth. Issie is becoming more confident in helping stroke patients when they arrive, but is quickly reminded she has a lot to learn and experience to gain. A man is blue-lighted in to AandE after being knocked off his bike, while Caitlin deals with a patient who has been admitted after having seizures

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Oscar-nominated star of Schindlers List, Ralph Fiennes, who is on the sofa to talk about his play Grace Pervades. Comedy actress Anna Faris returns to the world of Scary Movie for the next instalment in the spoof-horror franchise. Olivia Cooke, star of Amazon Primes The Girlfriend, talks about the upcoming series in the Game of Thrones prequel, House of the Dragon, and comedian Michelle de Swarte tells Claudia about her stand-up tour The Afters

programme.title: The Big Interview
programme.desc: Colette Fitzpatrick meets Kathleen Chada, whose husband murdered her young sons Eoghan and Ruairi in 2013

If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: My Hoarder Mum and Me
programme.desc: A Place in the Sun presenter Jasmine Harman embarks on a personal journey to help her mother Vasoulla overcome her extreme hoarding habit. Over the course of 30 years, the familys five-bedroom London home has become filled from floor to ceiling with clutter, forcing Jasmines 12-year-old brother Cameron to move out. Debate still rages over whether hoarding should be classified as a mental illness, and Jasmine meets the families of other sufferers to learn how they deal with the problem. Narrated by Rupert Houseman

A man is blue-lighted in to AandE after being knocked off his bike, while Caitlin deals with a patient who has been admitted after having seizures

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Oscar-nominated star of Schindlers List, Ralph Fiennes, who is on the sofa to talk about his play Grace Pervades. Comedy actress Anna Faris returns to the world of Scary Movie for the next instalment in the spoof-horror franchise. Olivia Cooke, star of Amazon Primes The Girlfriend, talks about the upcoming series in the Game of Thrones prequel, House of the Dragon, and comedian Michelle de Swarte tells Claudia about her stand-up tour The Afters

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix

programme.title: My Hoarder Mum and Me
programme.desc: A Place in the Sun presenter Jasmine Harman embarks on a personal journey to help her mother Vasoulla overcome her extreme hoarding habit. Over the course of 30 years, the familys five-bedroom London home has become filled from floor to ceiling with clutter, forcing Jasmines 12-year-old brother Cameron to move out. Debate still rages over whether hoarding should be classified as a mental illness, and Jasmine meets the families of other sufferers to learn how they deal with the problem. Narrated by Rupert Houseman

programme.title: The Night Caller
programme.desc: Rob advises Tony to leave town, revealing Ste Murray is involved with dangerous people - but shortly after the taxi driver is stunned when Lawrence hails his cab, stunned that he is finally getting to meet his hero. But he gets a shock to realise the radio presenter is the complete opposite of his public persona

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe prepares to take up his new post in London, but secretly regrets the decision to leave the Yorkshire Moors. However, the arrival of his replacement PC Wetherby signals it is too late to change his mind. Nurse Carol is administering vaccinations at the school when she notices extensive bruising on a boys body and suspects he is being abused. The youngster lives with his mother and stepfather, but recently his biological father has been back in touch, trying to see the boy - something the courts will not allow. However, tragedy strikes when conflict grows between the angry father and stepfather - with one of the Aidensfield bobbies caught in the crossfire. Rupert Ward-Lewis joins the cast

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York City is building an $11 billion construction project to extend the busy Long Island Railroad, and using brand-new technology, engineers and builders blast bedrock to create a vast tunnel below Grand Central Terminal

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The reception team face a change of pace as the holiday season ends. But even if things are quiet, Miguel still has his hands full with maintenance work. Mario has a mystery to solve before some VIP guests check in, while a new entertainer finds himself performing on the graveyard day shift, and sets out to prove his worth by taking charge of a bingo game

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwins historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than hes ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The WI members of Great Paxford live under a shadow as the Battle of Britain looms on the horizon. Pat befriends a Czech soldier, but finds her newfound independence less enjoyable than expected. Laura faces the consequences of her affair, while Sarah receives some shocking news. Alison strikes a bargain with the police, and tragedy strikes for Frances

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A hit-and-run incident alerts Nick to the presence of badger-baiters in the village, but the outsiders from Middlesbrough will stop at nothing in pursuit of their sport. Meanwhile, Greengrass finds a way of making racehorse ownership pay

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Xand van Tulleken, dietician Hala El-Shafie and cook Stacie Stewart oversee proceedings as three more pairs road test some of the most popular and talked about diets. Bus driver John and childrens entertainer Mark want to shed the pounds for a getaway to Florida, Kanan and Parul are desperate to get slimmer for a wedding, and Angela and Jacinta plan to shed some pounds for a charity run, while Xand investigates why alcohol is so harmful to waistlines and analyses how accurately fitness trackers count calories and log activities

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Harrogate Motel in North Yorkshire, where the owners are deliberately not telling their guests that the establishment is situated in the middle of a muddy equine centre. Mother and son duo Jennifer and Drew Birtwistle are horse lovers first and hoteliers second, but despite providing five-star accommodation for horses, the bedrooms are leaving the guests far from happy. Alex steps in and demands the owners start being more upfront about what they are offering, and tries to turn the horse-loving hosts into hospitality pros

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kerry wades in, while Robert gets an idea and Pollard is left with food for thought

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah sneaks round to see Todd while Theo is out, Jodie reveals her mischief to Gemma, Nick and Ben decide to get their sons together, and Ronnie offers Carl an olive branch

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: An expectant mother attends for treatment, but is harassed by her former husband. Stella is relieved to find she has not contracted an infection from her attacker, but suffers accusations of lying to save her reputation. Meanwhile, Sister Brigid makes a misguided suggestion, and a surprise visitor from overseas forces Goodwin to reconsider his future plans

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Poirot meets a woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, Inspector Frost is called on to investigate a suspicious death at a power station and the murder of a fellow detective _ leading to an unpleasant clash of egos with the Crime Squads acerbic chief. Popular detective drama, starring David Jason, with Bruce Alexander, Paul Jesson, Nicky Henson and Beth Goddard

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Tongues start wagging when Greengrass goes into business with a stranger from America, while Kate has her work cut out looking after a flu-stricken Nick. Rural drama, starring Bill Maynard and Nick Berry

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Return of the crime drama, starring Suranne Jones and Rose Leslie. When a British Air Force weapons test goes disastrously wrong, DCI Amy Silva is called in to investigate, and finds herself caught in the crosshairs of a fraught international incident. The Air Force think they have the guilty party in custody and push for Amy and DI Kirsten Longacre to close the case. But Amy isnt convinced

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah quizzes Danielle about Theos violent streak, Bernie hears promising news of Mals future plans, George meets an estate agent, and Carl gives Summer a clue to the identity of Billys killer

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert decides it is time to come clean, while Dawn is shocked and Kerry apologises

programme.title: Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Helen Mirren stars in the last-ever episode of the ground-breaking police drama. Jane tries desperately to locate Penny Philips, who has been taken hostage by murder suspect Curtis Flynn. However, the detective finds herself increasingly drained as her last case takes plenty of twists and turns before reaching its shattering conclusion. Stephen Tompkinson, Robert Pugh and Gary Lewis co-star

programme.title: The Night Caller
programme.desc: A vengeful Tony creeps into Lawrences house to face him. Lawrence thinks hes being burgled, until he recognises the intruder as the cabbie who picked him up earlier that night. He heads for the kitchen to call the police but theres a scuffle. Tony grabs a kitchen knife and Lawrence is forced to do what he says. Thriller, starring Robert Glenister

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jennys behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victims widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesnt count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jennys life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Affair at the Victory Ball
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friends estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon is devastated, while Cain is rocked and Kerry is riddled with guilt

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rhona has a big decision to make, while Cain confides in Moira and Kerry is furious

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain is involved in an accident, while Jacob is left humiliated and Kerry confides in Jai

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kyle finds Cain lying unconscious, while Kerry starts to have suspicions and Moira is concerned

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kerry wades in, while Robert gets an idea and Pollard is left with food for thought

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert decides it is time to come clean, while Dawn is shocked and Kerry apologises

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans directs this episode, set against the backdrop of the first moon landings in 1969. The detective investigates the deaths of an astrophysicist and his girlfriend in a tragic car accident as the rest of the world eagerly awaits news from Apollo 11. When he begins to suspect foul play, Endeavour enlists Thursdays help in getting to the truth, visiting the observatory where the dead man worked and discovering the possibility that he was not the intended victim. The case is complicated further when the dead scientist is revealed to have been part of a mysterious cult-like group

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: The Michelin-starred chef explores new possibilities at his East Sussex smallholding, heading off to the Knepp in Horsham to learn all about different tomato varieties in its impressive market garden. After losing some of his ducks to foxes, Marcus is determined to keep the rest safe and local expert Tom. has interesting and unexpected ideas, including introducing some noisy new arrivals

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the sun rises on the Costa Blanca, the Hotel Benidorm Plazas housekeeping staff spring into action. Meanwhile, expat Jessie has an audition to be the new hotel entertainer, and cameras follow the Parrot family as they head to Benidorm to spend some precious family time together

programme.title: The Secret Life of Our Pets
programme.desc: How domesticated animals use their senses to navigate the human world, from dogs with thermal sensing noses and cats with the ability to navigate using magnetic forces, to masses of flocking birds who use their senses to fly within millimetres from one another. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson helps sisters Sharon and Joy with their struggling country house hotel in Worlington, Suffolk. While the Grade-II listed building is in good repair, the operating systems are chaotic and the menu too extensive for the solitary chef to handle. Ruth organises a revamp of the dated-looking function room - prompting the delighted owners to plan a wedding fair in their new-look space

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Workers digging up a path by Hove lagoon unearth a dark secret that was meant to stay buried for good. Meanwhile, what appears to be an isolated missing-persons incident across the city soon becomes an investigation bigger than Grace could have imagined when a chilling detail linking the lagoon discovery and the missing-person case leads to a hunt for a serial offender. Crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: To Die For (1995)
programme.desc: An ambitious small town woman has a lifelong obsession of becoming a TV star. A job at a local TV station seems like the first step on her journey to the top, but when her husband announces his desire to start a family, she realises he has to go and persuades her dim-witted teenage lover to murder him. Gus Van Sants black comedy, starring Nicole Kidman, Matt Dillon and Joaquin Phoenix

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: The unit investigates the links between a crime lord and a deranged artist who may have been using his skills to create something illegal. Meanwhile, Atwater and Burgess are assigned the seemingly routine task of dealing with a hoarder whose collection is upsetting some of her neighbours - but they discover something shocking amid the clutter. Crime drama, starring Jason Beghe and Sophia Bush

programme.title: The Chase Australia
programme.desc: Andrew OKeefe presents the Down Under version of the quiz show in which four people must work together to beat the Chaser and win a cash prize

programme.title: C*A*U*G*H*T
programme.desc: The boys story starts to gain traction online, raising the ire of the officials who sent them there, but attracting disgraced journalist Josie Justice to rebuild her career on the back of their growing fame

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by national treasure Stephen Fry, star of The White Lotus and Sex Education Aimee Lou Wood, BAFTA Rising Star nominee Sope Dirisu and comedian Joanne McNally. Plus, a musical performance from Yungblud

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Shaun, Gina, John and Jane

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Sam, Sharna, Gary and Sandy

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Josh, Scott and Tim go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Sarah from Lancashire.

programme.title: Storage Hoarders
programme.desc: Aggie MacKenzie is joined by antiques expert Tom Keane to help two more compulsive collectors - one whose possessions are locked away in a storage unit and another whose passion for cufflinks and other memorabilia has spiralled out of control

programme.title: The Restaurant
programme.desc: Broadcaster, podcaster and social media star Doireann Garrihy takes her turn as the guest chef. From childhood memories to favourite dishes, Doireann is hoping that her menu can gain more stars than two previous head chefs - her sister Aoibheann and her radio colleague Donncha OCallaghan

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, passenger experience manager Demi deals with a medical emergency. Meanwhile, border force officer Rob returns to cross-examine unsuspecting passengers arriving into the UK

programme.title: Live SSE Airtricity Mens Premier Division
programme.sub-title: St Patricks Athletic v Shamrock Rovers
programme.desc: St Patricks Athletic v Shamrock Rovers (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the match at Richmond Park. St Pats missed out on the European places last term after finishing fifth, while Rovers won the title, but there has looked to be little between the two sides in the early stages of the current campaign

programme.title: Bump
programme.desc: Reema comes back to Australia, and Vince has to deal with the realisation that she might not have come back for him. Rosa, jealous of Matiass new twin daughters, decides to throw Oly the best baby shower ever

programme.title: Bump
programme.desc: Oly, who didnt have the chance to prepare for the birth of her first child, is struggling to cope with her anxiety and independence this time around

programme.title: C*A*U*G*H*T
programme.desc: Dim-witted Australian soldiers Phil Choi, Dylan Fox, Rowdy Gaines and Albhanis Mouawad undertake a covert mission on behalf of the minister of defence. The luckless quartet are captured by freedom fighters on the small island nation and make a hostage video that they hope will go viral. The soldiers unexpectedly become global celebrities and the captives wonder if their dire predicament could be the best thing that ever happened to them. Comedy, starring Ben OToole, Kick Gurry and Lincoln Younes

programme.title: Iain Stirlings CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling presents the game show in which five contestants take on five famous faces - Jennifer Metcalfe, James Argent, Marcel Sommerville, Paisley Billings and Jordan Davies - in a series of challenges based on unusual abilities that the celebrities claim they possess

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: The aftermath of a dramatic kiss-off rocks the masseria to its core. Some girls question whether they made the right decision. One couple judges the decision made by their friend, another continues to battle their communication issues, and a third grapples with nerves and uncertainty

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Emotions run high as the girls return from their dates. One wonders whether she missed her shot, while another struggles with the partner swapping, and a secret crush gets exposed

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined in the studio by actor Pierce Brosnan, Severence director and stars Ben Stiller, Adam Scott and Britt Lower, and comedians John Bishop and Judi Love. With a musical performance from AJ Tracey and Jorja Smith

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.desc: Jann and her sister come together after finding out about their mothers diagnosis. Comedy, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: A review of the latest matches in Europes premier club competition, plus a look ahead to forthcoming fixtures

programme.title: Britains Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britains Brightest Family. In the fifth episode, the Baker family from Wales and the Sheth family from London battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Culinary expert Frances Atkins tests three home cooks who are tasked with making a German pasta called spaetzle. Sheree Murphy hosts

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Pop star and 911 lead singer Lee Brennan prepares a carefully selected menu for a dinner party, with each dish designed to provide his oblivious guests with clues relating to their hosts identity. Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Flint family from Hertfordshire swap homes, budgets and lives with the Elayadi family from Ealing, and, though they live just 13 miles away from each other, their financial circumstances are worlds apart. The Elayadis live in a two-bedroom council flat together with their three children and their weekly budget after bills is around £135 per week, while Sam and Richard Flint are among the richest 10 per cent in Britain

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Ryans take on Ladies Day at the Galway Races, Car Lovers restore a 21-year-old vehicle to its former glory and Lukes window cleaning team pursue the perfect streak-free shine

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: Wales v Scotland (Kick-off 4.40pm). Coverage of the final match in the opening round of fixtures, held at Principality Stadium. Scotland claimed a narrow 24-21 home win when the teams met in this competition last year, as well as a more convincing 38-8 victory in Salford in the World Cup last August. The Scots also prevailed 20-18 on Welsh soil in 2024, but Sean Lynns side will be eager to end that run of defeats this time around

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern is in Sheffield as he hosts the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Mark Charnock, Kerry Godliman and Janet Street-Porter all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: A Sheffield pawnbroker takes a helicopter ride to the biggest deal of his career - and sees another chance to make a profit when a customer brings in a Rolex she bought for £25 in a car boot sale. John Freeze faces a tough choice when offered a collection of 20 designer handbags, knowing that the fickle nature of fashion can make their value highly changeable. Ray takes delivery of a bespoke, hand-made Rolls Royce - but with the wedding industry it was made for at a standstill due to Covid-19, buyers may be in short supply

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to revisit some of the most memorable moments from Gogglebox Ireland, demonstrating why Irelands resident telly addicts have developed a reputation for eccentricity. Includes highlights featuring the Tully twins, Angela and Eileen, Anita and Tracey, the Adenugas and the Ryans

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as contestants Daniel, Rebekah, Raymond, Samuel and Daniella face the six Chasers - the Sinnerman, the Beast, the Governess, the Menace, the Vixen and the Dark Destroyer, arguably the greatest quiz team in the world

programme.title: Dark Side of Reality TV
programme.desc: Twenty women are deceived into believing their would-be suitor is worth millions, and made to look like gold diggers in front of 70 million viewers

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: It all comes to a head as old adversaries clash, with the fate of New Eden hanging in the balance

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
